name: azure-cloud-migrate
description: Assess and migrate cross-cloud workloads to Azure. Generates assessment reports and converts code from AWS, GCP, or other providers to Azure services. WHEN: \"migrate Lambda to Azure Functions\", \"migrate AWS to Azure\", \"Lambda migration assessment\", \"convert AWS serverless to Azure\", \"migration readiness report\", \"migrate from AWS\", \"migrate from GCP\", \"cross-cloud migration\".
capabilities: Generate comprehensive migration assessment reports comparing AWS Lambda to Azure Functions
Convert AWS Lambda code to Azure Functions with proper bindings and triggers
Map AWS services (S3, DynamoDB, SQS, SNS) to Azure equivalents (Blob Storage, Cosmos DB, Service Bus)
Create architecture diagrams for current AWS and target Azure states
Follow Azure Functions best practices including identity-first authentication and bindings over SDKs
Support multiple runtimes: Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, C#, Java, PowerShell
author: microsoft
securitySummary: This is an official Microsoft skill for migrating AWS Lambda workloads to Azure Functions. Static findings are false positives: backtick usage is markdown code formatting, environment variable access is legitimate Azure authentication, network URLs point to Azure documentation, and the SAM detection refers to AWS Serverless Application Model, not Windows Security Accounts Manager. No malicious code detected.
valueStatement: This skill assesses and migrates AWS Lambda workloads to Azure Functions, generating detailed migration reports and converting code with proper bindings and best practices.
userTitle: Migrate AWS Lambda to Azure Functions
limitations: Does not execute actual cloud resource deployment (hand off to azure-prepare skill)
Does not migrate stateful data - only code and configuration
Does not handle Lambda layers or container image migrations
Does not support migration to Azure Container Apps or other serverless options

useCases.targetUser: Enterprise cloud architects and DevOps teams
useCases.title: Enterprise Cloud Migration
useCases.description: Large organization migrating multiple AWS Lambda functions to Azure Functions for cost optimization and Azure integration

useCases.title: Development Team Porting
useCases.description: Development team moving from AWS to Azure and needing to quickly convert serverless functions

useCases.title: Multi-Cloud Assessment
useCases.description: Technical assessment comparing AWS Lambda and Azure Functions capabilities for new serverless projects

promptTemplates.title: Basic Migration Request
promptTemplates.prompt: Migrate my AWS Lambda function in folder [folder-name] to Azure Functions
promptTemplates.scenario: Simple migration of a single Lambda function

promptTemplates.title: Full Assessment with Code
promptTemplates.prompt: Run the full migration assessment on my AWS Lambda project in [folder-path] and then migrate all functions to Azure Functions

promptTemplates.title: Specific Runtime Migration
promptTemplates.prompt: Migrate my Python Lambda functions in [folder] to Azure Functions using Python v2 programming model

promptTemplates.title: Architecture Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a migration assessment report for my serverless application showing current AWS architecture and target Azure architecture with diagrams

bestPractices: Always run assessment phase before code migration to understand migration scope
Use identity-based authentication (Managed Identity) instead of API keys or connection strings
Prefer Azure Functions bindings over SDKs for better performance and smaller footprint
Use the latest supported runtime versions for Azure Functions
antiPatterns: Skipping assessment phase and going directly to code migration
Using connection strings or API keys instead of Managed Identity
Creating manual function.json files when using v4 JavaScript or v2 Python model
Defaulting to older runtime versions instead of latest GA versions

faq.question: Does this skill delete my original AWS code?
faq.answer: No. The skill creates a new output directory with Azure Functions code and never modifies the original source directory.

faq.question: What runtimes are supported?
faq.answer: Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, C#, Java, and PowerShell are supported with their respective Azure Functions programming models.

faq.question: Does this skill deploy to Azure?
faq.answer: No. The skill generates migration assessment and code. Use the azure-prepare skill for infrastructure deployment.

faq.question: How does this handle environment variables?
faq.answer: The assessment maps Lambda environment variables to Azure Functions app settings, recommending Managed Identity for authentication.

faq.question: Can I migrate Lambda layers?
faq.answer: No. Lambda layers require manual migration. The skill will identify dependencies that need separate handling.

faq.question: What triggers are supported?
faq.answer: HTTP, Blob, Queue, Service Bus, Timer, and Event Grid triggers are supported with equivalent Azure Functions bindings.
